The trail is faint in places and marked with ribbons along several route options, making for a quiet hike through the hills, though the summit view is somewhat limited.

114518.description_ja_short: 熊本県宇土半島の先端、標高406m。天翔台から有明海や雲仙を一望できる山。
114518.description_en_long: Misumidake stands 406 meters at the tip of the Uto Peninsula in Uki City, Kumamoto Prefecture. Its ridge runs north to south, and at the southern end the Tensho Platform exposes andesite rock with sweeping views across the Ariake and Yatsushiro Seas to Unzen and Shimabara in the distance.
114518.description_en_short: A 406-meter peak at the tip of the Uto Peninsula, Kumamoto, with sweeping sea views from Tensho Platform.

114928.description_en_long: Byobudake is a 394-meter mountain in Nagasaki Prefecture. The prefecture has many similar low mountains, and no detailed records of this particular peak's trails or name origin could be found. Given its modest elevation, it can be regarded as a satoyama-type hill.
114928.description_en_short: A 394-meter mountain in Nagasaki Prefecture, one of many similar low hills with few detailed records.

114956.description_en_long: Koshidake is a 487.7-meter mountain in the southern part of Imari City, Saga Prefecture, on the Kitamatsuura Peninsula. It yields a glassy obsidian known as karasunmakura, whose stone tools have been found at prehistoric sites across and beyond Kyushu.

114975.description_ja_short: 長崎県宇久島にある標高258mの山。五島列島を一望できる島内随一の展望地である。
114975.description_en_long: Rising 258 meters at the center of Uku Island in Nagasaki Prefecture, Shirogadake is regarded as the island's premier viewpoint, offering a sweeping panorama of the Goto Islands. Within Saikai National Park, its summit also preserves a former Imperial Japanese Navy radar station, recognized as a Japan Heritage site tied to the Sasebo Naval District.
114975.description_en_short: A 258-meter peak at the heart of Uku Island, Nagasaki, prized as the island's best viewpoint over the Goto Islands.
